note: IB Diploma Programme Group 2 — Language Acquisition. Two courses: Language B (for students with prior experience) and Language ab initio (beginners). Most common languages: French, Spanish, Mandarin, Japanese, German. Assessed via internal oral and external written exams.

topics.subject: Language B
topics.description: Themes: lifestyle, health, beliefs, subcultures. Students discuss and write about personal experiences and cultural identity in the target language.

topics.subject: Language B
topics.subtopicTitle: Learning and leisure, travel, and migration
topics.description: Themes: education systems, travel, global movement of people. Students compare and discuss these experiences across cultures.

topics.subject: Language B
topics.description: Themes: innovation, technology, arts and expression. Students analyse how these shape modern society using target language texts.

topics.subject: Language B
topics.subtopicTitle: Community, social structures, and political systems
topics.description: Themes: social groups, rights and responsibilities, governance. Students engage with a range of texts and perspectives.

topics.subject: Language B
topics.description: Themes: environment, global challenges, peace and conflict, ethical questions. Assessment: Paper 1 (reading comprehension); Paper 2 (written tasks); Individual Oral (stimulus-based).

topics.subject: Language ab initio
topics.subtopicTitle: Beginner language acquisition across three themes
topics.description: For students with no prior experience of the language. Five themes (same as Language B) covered at beginner to intermediate level. Focus on receptive, productive, and interactive skills. Assessed by Paper 1 (reading/listening), Paper 2 (writing), and Individual Oral.
